lesson.menu_desc: Polite questions & landmark directions on foot -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 11 -- Walking + Asking Directions: Finding Your Way on Foot
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Roberto Pires | Aula 11 -- Walking + Asking Directions

listenings.text: Hello there. You look a little lost, so let me help you find your way. To reach the cathedral, go straight along this street for about two blocks. When you pass a small fountain on your right, keep walking until you reach a big square. The cathedral is right next to the square, opposite a bakery. It is not far at all, only a five-minute walk. If you get lost again, just look for the tall stone tower. You can see it from almost everywhere. Have a lovely walk and enjoy the old town.

When I first arrived in Barcelona, I was afraid to ask anyone for help. I just walked around in circles and got more and more lost. One day, I finally stopped a kind local and asked, could you tell me how to get to the museum. To my surprise, she smiled and explained everything slowly. After that, I asked for directions every single day. I learned that most people are happy to help a polite traveler. Now, getting lost on foot is part of the adventure. I always find a friendly person who points me the right way.

extra_audio.text: First, Roberto stops a friendly local and asks politely how to get to the cathedral. Then the local tells him to go straight ahead for two blocks. Next, she explains that he should keep walking until he reaches the main square. After that, she points to the tall tower he can use as a landmark. Finally, Roberto thanks her and walks off in the right direction.
